name: Christmas Gingerbread Bowl and Cookies Pt. 2
recipeIngredient: Additional unspecified ingredients (as listed in part one)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream butter and sugars together until fluffy for about 1 minute.
2. Add molasses, corn syrup, vanilla, and eggs to the mixture one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. In another bowl, whisk together remaining ingredients.
4. Gradually combine flour mixture with creamed mixture (dough will be stiff; use hands if needed).
5. Divide dough into quarters and wrap in plastic wrap. Chill overnight.
6. Cover outside of 1 1/2 quart ovenproof glass bowl with aluminum foil, ensuring it's smooth. Spray the entire bowl with vegetable cooking spray.
7. Roll one-fourth of the dough into a circle about 1/4 inch thick. Press onto the bowl's exterior, molding firmly without stretching. Trim excess dough around the edge using a sharp knife. Using a small star-shaped cutter, make stars at intervals along the lipped edge, about 1 1/2 to 2 inches apart and 1 1/2 inches from the bottom.
8. Refrigerate the bowl for an hour to firm up the dough.
9. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Place inverted bowl on a greased baking sheet and bake 20-30 minutes or until lightly browned and firm. Let cool, then carefully remove foil and gingerbread shell from the bowl, discarding foil.
10. Use remaining dough to create additional bowls or cut into cookies using decorative Christmas cutters. Bake cookies for 8-10 minutes on a greased baking sheet until lightly browned.
